creator: mwm@mired.org
text:
Will Andrews <will@physics.purdue.edu> types:
> On Sun, Sep 02, 2001 at 12:03:45PM -0500, Mike Meyer (mwm@mired.org) wrote:
> > diff -u Makefile.orig Makefile
> > --- Makefile.orig	Sun Sep  2 11:48:10 2001
> > +++ Makefile	Sun Sep  2 12:01:35 2001
> > @@ -107,3 +107,6 @@
> >  .else
> >  	@${ECHO_MSG} "Error: Please define either SUP_UPDATE or CVS_UPDATE first."
> >  .endif
> > +.if defined(UPDATE_PORTS_INDEX)
> > +	make index
> > +.endif
> I forgot to mention.  This patch is completely pointless since
> you can just put the target on the command line.

Not when it's invoked via make update in /usr/src, which will run
"make update" if /etc/make.conf is set up properly.

> And "make"
> should be ${MAKE}...  and I already told you why INDEX is just
> slightly out of date.  It'll be updated for the release...

In that case, it needs to be fixed in the index target. I was
surprised a bit surprised to see that index used a bar "make", but
figured the author knew what they were doing and did it their way.

	<mike
